generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT occupancy sensor deployed in physical building to capture real-time occupancy data for adaptive control systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataStream
]

instance TemperatureHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT environmental sensor measuring temperature and humidity conditions in building zones for HVAC optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataStream
]

instance LuminositySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT luminosity sensor capturing light levels in building spaces for adaptive lighting control systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataStream
]

instance PostOccupancyFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy sensor providing feedback for reinforcement learning-based optimization of building systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyFeedbackDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureHumiditySensor
]

instance LightLevelDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LuminositySensor
]

instance OccupancyFeedbackDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PostOccupancyFeedbackSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Information Model serving as semantic backbone enriched with runtime semantics for devices, sensors, and control logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToRuntimeTransformer
]

instance RuntimeSemanticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM model with device metadata (tenant/device IDs, endpoint addresses) for DT deployment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoSynchronizer
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time data streams from IoT sensors and control policies"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataStream, EnvironmentalDataStream, LightLevelDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoIngestor
]

instance HistoricalBuildingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical logs and performance data from building systems for predictive analytics"
]

instance ControlPolicyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning models and adaptive control policies for building systems"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyFeedbackDataStream
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoIngestor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono providing lightweight M2M communication for real-time data ingestion from physical sensors"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance EclipseDittoSynchronizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto enabling digital thread management with bidirectional synchronization and standardized data models"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalThreadService
]

instance BimToRuntimeTransformer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven engineering tool for automated extraction of BIM semantics and generation of runtime interfaces"
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 delivering rich, interactive 3D visualizations for monitoring and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance ReinforcementLearningOptimizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning framework (e.g., BOREALIS) enabling dynamic optimization of building systems"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time monitoring and visualization of building operational state via Unreal Engine 5"
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingStateInsight, SystemAlertInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing predictive analytics and optimization using reinforcement learning models"
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling simulation and what-if analysis for life-cycle optimization"
    DTDFVocab:provides RefurbishmentScenarioInsight, EnergyEfficiencyScenarioInsight
]

instance DigitalThreadService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing digital thread synchronization between physical and virtual representations"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateSynchronizationAction, ControlPolicyUpdateAction
]

instance AdaptiveLightingControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service controlling adaptive lighting systems using reinforcement learning for energy-visual comfort trade-offs"
    DTDFVocab:provides LightingOptimizationAction, EnergyVisualComfortBalanceAction
]

instance CustomAnalyticsIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling custom domain-specific analytics via standardized extension points"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight, PredictiveMaintenanceRecommendation
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational insights showing current system states (energy consumption, occupancy patterns) via Unreal Engine visualizations"
]

instance SystemAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alerts and notifications about system anomalies or operational thresholds being exceeded"
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive recommendations for optimal control policies balancing energy consumption and visual comfort"
]

instance OccupancyPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analyzed occupancy patterns informing space utilization and energy management strategies"
]

instance RefurbishmentScenarioInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation-based insights for refurbishment planning and energy efficiency improvements"
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyScenarioInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "What-if analysis results for different energy efficiency scenarios"
]

instance LightingOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of lighting systems based on real-time occupancy and luminosity data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyVisualComfortBalanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic balancing of energy consumption and visual comfort using reinforcement learning"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemStateSynchronizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Bidirectional synchronization ensuring DT reflects current physical system state"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlPolicyUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic deployment of updated control policies to physical building systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of system faults or performance degradation through real-time monitoring"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for maintenance actions based on predictive analytics of system health"
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for lighting fixtures configured via BIM-enriched models"
]

instance HvacDamperActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for HVAC dampers with spatial relationships defined in BIM"
]

instance MotorizedBlindsActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for motorized blinds with control logic from BIM models"
]

instance PlcController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Programmable Logic Controller executing commands from DT to adjust physical building systems"
]

instance IoTGatewayController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT gateway facilitating bidirectional communication between DT and physical actuators"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world include:
  - **Control commands** generated by the DT (e.g., lighting adjustments, HVAC setpoints) transmitted back to the **Building Control System (BCS)** via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**.
  - **Actuator commands** executed by physical devices (e.g., dimming lights, adjusting dampers) based on **real-time optimization** (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning for energy-visual comfort trade-offs).
  - **Automated commissioning** of devices, where the DT’s **runtime interfaces** (generated from BIM) enable seamless integration of new sensors/actuators without manual reconfiguration."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time synchronization** time scale, ensuring continuous bidirectional communication between the physical and virtual domains. The system is designed for **low-latency updates** to maintain alignment with operational changes in the physical building. While specific latency metrics (e.g., sub-second) are not detailed, the framework emphasizes **event-driven updates** and **continuous synchronization** to support dynamic control and monitoring. The **BOREALIS project** exemplifies this with iterative improvements to control policies using real-time sensor feedback."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented architecture** with the following components:
  - **Central DT Framework**: Acts as the **hub** for integrating BIM, real-time data, and services.
  - **Middleware Layer**: **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** handle **data normalization, routing, and synchronization** between PT and DT.
  - **Visualization Layer**: **Unreal Engine 5** provides **immersive monitoring** and **interactive simulations**.
  - **Extension Points**: Standardized interfaces for **third-party services** (e.g., analytics, AI/ML models) to plug into the DT.
  - **Modular Services**: **Lighting control**, **HVAC optimization**, and **predictive maintenance** are deployed as **independent modules** within the constellation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the following life-cycle stages of the physical building:
  - **Design phase**: Leverages **BIM models** (static representations) as the foundation for DT deployment.
  - **Construction phase**: Integrates **as-built data** (e.g., sensor placements, device configurations) into the DT via **model-driven transformations**.
  - **Operation phase**: Enables **real-time synchronization** with the PT, supporting **adaptive control** (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning) and **predictive maintenance**.
  - **Refurbishment/Adaptation**: Facilitates **life-cycle optimization** by simulating changes (e.g., retrofits) before physical implementation. The DT represents the PT in its **as-operated state**, with continuous updates to reflect **wear, degradation, or reconfigurations**."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**: Quantifies the **alignment between PT and DT** by evaluating **data consistency, synchronization latency, and semantic coherence**.
  - **Semantic drift mitigation**: The framework uses **bidirectional synchronization** (via Eclipse Ditto) to **minimize divergence** between PT and DT states.
  - **Validation mechanisms**: **Automated checks** (e.g., model validation [65]) ensure BIM metadata is correctly transformed into runtime artifacts.
  - **Uncertainty handling**: **Probabilistic models** (e.g., in BOREALIS) account for **sensor noise** and **control policy uncertainties** during optimization."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT employs **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as middleware for **real-time data exchange** between physical assets and the virtual twin. These tools provide **standardized interfaces** for bidirectional communication, supporting **event-driven updates** and **low-latency synchronization**. While specific network protocols (e.g., HTTP, MQTT) are not detailed, the framework ensures **interoperability** with Building Control Systems (BCS) and IoT devices. The connection is designed to handle **high-frequency data streams** from sensors and actuators, enabling dynamic control and monitoring."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed using a **model-driven automation framework** that leverages BIM as the foundational model. The deployment process is **scalable and reproducible**, with runtime artifacts generated via **model transformations** and validated using the **ODTE metric**. The system is demonstrated in a **Living Lab case study**, where the DT is hosted in a **service-oriented architecture** with standardized extension points. While specific hosting technologies (e.g., Cloud, Edge, Docker) are not mentioned, the framework supports **modular and extensible** deployments tailored to AECO requirements."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT supports **horizontal integration** with external systems through its **collaborative BIM platform**, which facilitates coordination among **architects, engineers, and construction professionals**. While specific integrations (e.g., ERP, CMMS) are not detailed, the framework emphasizes **stakeholder alignment** and **data sharing** to enhance interoperability. The **model-driven approach** ensures compatibility with AECO-specific standards, enabling seamless exchange of information across disciplines."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT framework adheres to **BIM as the standard solution** in the AECO domain, ensuring compatibility with existing **AECO-specific systems and standards**. While specific standards (e.g., IFC, COBie) are not explicitly listed, the system emphasizes **semantic integration** and **interoperability** through **model-driven engineering (MDE)** and **standardized extension points**. The use of **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** aligns with open-source middleware standards for real-time data exchange."
]
